key_insight: A third-medium contact formulation can be stabilized by treating an auxiliary deformation-gradient-like field as an independent variable and regularizing it with a gradient penalty, avoiding direct higher-order derivatives of displacement.
engineering_relevance: This gives a smooth and robust alternative to classical contact constraints for large-deformation contact and self-contact problems, with practical benefit even when using low-order displacement elements.
method: Introduce an auxiliary deformation-gradient-like field in the compliant third medium.
Couple the auxiliary field to the physical deformation gradient with a penalty term.
Add a gradient-based regularization on the auxiliary field; test continuous and element-wise discontinuous interpolation, with local condensation possible for discontinuous fields.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmark evaluations on large deformation, progressive self-contact, and severe third-medium compression, showing effective stabilization especially for continuous low-order auxiliary fields.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on the choice of auxiliary-field interpolation and penalty/regularization parameters, so calibration and conditioning should be checked in implementation.
paper_title: A robust mixed finite element formulation for third medium contact
